title: Quiz: Interest Rate Derivatives
description: Test your understanding of swaps, caps, floors, and rate modeling

questions.question: In a plain vanilla interest rate swap, what is exchanged?

options.text: Fixed rate for floating rate payments

questions.explanation: A vanilla interest rate swap exchanges fixed-rate interest payments for floating-rate payments based on a reference rate like SOFR. Principal is notional and is not exchanged.

questions.question: What is the par swap rate?

options.text: The rate that makes the swap value zero
The rate set by the central bank
The average of fixed and floating rates

questions.explanation: The par swap rate is the fixed rate that makes the present value of the fixed leg equal to the present value of the floating leg, giving the swap a value of zero at initiation.

questions.question: What is an interest rate cap?

options.text: A portfolio of caplets (call options on forward rates)

questions.explanation: An interest rate cap is a series of caplets, each being a call option on a forward rate. The cap pays off when the floating rate exceeds the cap strike rate.

questions.question: What is a payer swaption?

options.text: An option to receive fixed in a swap
An option to pay fixed in a swap
A swap that only pays once

questions.explanation: A payer swaption gives the holder the right to enter into a swap where they PAY the fixed rate. It benefits from rising interest rates because paying fixed becomes more attractive when floating rates rise.

questions.question: Mean-reversion means interest rates tend to return toward a long-term level over time.
questions.explanation: True. Mean-reversion is a key property in interest rate models like Vasicek and Hull-White. When rates are high, they tend to fall; when low, they tend to rise, pulling toward a long-term average.

questions.question: What is the annuity factor in swaption pricing?

options.text: The sum of discount factors for swap payment dates

questions.explanation: The annuity factor (or swap annuity) is the sum of discounted year fractions for all swap payment dates. It converts rate differences into present value and is crucial for swaption pricing.

questions.question: What does Black's formula price in the context of interest rates?
questions.explanation: Black's formula (Black-76) is the standard model for pricing European-style interest rate options including caps, floors, and swaptions, treating forward rates as the underlying.

questions.question: Which are properties of the Vasicek interest rate model? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Has analytical solutions for bond prices

questions.explanation: The Vasicek model features mean-reversion, can produce negative rates (since it uses normal distribution), and has closed-form solutions for zero-coupon bond prices. It does NOT guarantee positive rates.
